sourceMovie.title: The Castle of Cagliostro
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit The Castle of Cagliostro for the same reason it broke through in 1979: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a adventure rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Hayao Miyazaki keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The animation and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Lupin III: The First
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Castle of Cagliostro clicked for you, Lupin III: The First (2019) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and adventure, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If The Castle of Cagliostro clicked for you, Cars (2006) is a strong next stop: both trade in animation and adventure, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after The Castle of Cagliostro?
faq.answer: Try Lupin III: The First, Cars, One Piece Film: GOLD—each shares DNA with The Castle of Cagliostro while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is The Castle of Cagliostro?
faq.answer: The Castle of Cagliostro (1979) is best described as Animation, Adventure, Comedy, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is The Castle of Cagliostro part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Lupin the Third Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
